intent: Deliver one shared Launchdeck Application Kernel and Operation Registry behind a backward-compatible CLI, a typed local stdio MCP server, one canonical MCP-first Skill, and separately generated Codex and Claude Plugins with durable recovery and exact readiness evidence.

complete_first_scope_preservation.priority_label_policy: P1/P2/P3 labels order work; they do not defer delivery scope.

user_confirmed_deferrals.exact_excluded_behavior: First-release adoption.apply and every automatic project/user configuration write, registry mutation, or implicit task start.
user_confirmed_deferrals.residual_risk: Users receive inspection and a proposed plan only; adopting a project remains manual until a separately reviewed digest/confirmation contract exists.
user_confirmed_deferrals.reopen_or_stop_condition: Stop and reopen specification if adoption writes or apply become a required delivery outcome.
user_confirmed_deferrals.downstream_artifact: tasks.md must contain only adoption.inspect implementation and negative zero-write acceptance for this feature.

user_confirmed_deferrals.exact_excluded_behavior: First-release public MCP execution for medium-risk tasks.
user_confirmed_deferrals.residual_risk: Medium-risk configured tasks remain available only through existing CLI policy and are not an Agent fallback target.
user_confirmed_deferrals.reopen_or_stop_condition: Stop and reopen specification when a target host proves a portable human-elicitation contract and medium-risk Agent execution is requested.
user_confirmed_deferrals.downstream_artifact: tasks.md must implement and prove low-risk-only public MCP refusal, retaining the future digest-bound confirmation model as documentation only.

user_confirmed_deferrals.exact_excluded_behavior: Remote MCP/OAuth, hooks, MCP UI, GUI/TUI, native executable packaging, enterprise managed policy, and production/remote service control.
user_confirmed_deferrals.residual_risk: First-release use requires local Node 20+ and stdio host integration; no remote or visual management surface exists.
user_confirmed_deferrals.reopen_or_stop_condition: Stop and create a new specification if any deferred surface becomes a required delivery outcome or readiness claim.
user_confirmed_deferrals.downstream_artifact: tasks.md and release evidence must contain no implementation or readiness roll-up for these surfaces.

architecture_decisions.decision: Create `src/kernel/operation-registry.js` and `src/kernel/application-kernel.js` as the only shared execution and safety authority.
architecture_decisions.rationale: CLI and MCP must share semantics while existing control-plane modules remain resource truth.

architecture_decisions.decision: Declare exactly 18 first-release public Agent operations and generate/validate CLI mapping, MCP tools, Skill routing, capabilities, and contract cases from the catalog.
architecture_decisions.rationale: One declaration prevents surface and policy drift.

architecture_decisions.decision: Add a versioned operation journal at `<LAUNCHDECK_HOME>/runtime/operations/v1` with per-record atomic JSON, rebuildable creation indexes, existing lock semantics, and 30-day terminal retention.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Prepared-before-effects, same-ID recovery, and cross-surface reconciliation require durable shared evidence.

architecture_decisions.decision: Reduce `src/cli.js` to parsing/routing/presentation over Kernel operations while preserving CLI schemaVersion 1, text, compact projection, fields, statuses, exit codes, and CLI-only routes.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Agent protocol and CLI compatibility evolve independently.

architecture_decisions.decision: Implement MCP with pinned SDK v1.x, exact Zod, and `StdioServerTransport`; stdout is protocol-only and task output remains in bounded Launchdeck observations.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Use the standard protocol owner and protect host framing.

architecture_decisions.decision: Validate the exact closed nine-field AgentOperationResult and keep `outcome.kind` separate from `resource.status`.
architecture_decisions.rationale: All surfaces need stable semantic equivalence and explicit effects certainty.

architecture_decisions.decision: Resolve one configured project and re-evaluate current risk, ownership, compatibility, locks, and optional plan digest inside the Kernel immediately before mutation.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Host hints and MCP annotations are not authorization.

architecture_decisions.decision: Add a bounded observation service with opaque query-bound high-water cursors; public operation correlation remains <=15 minutes and <=20 results.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Logs/events/recovery must not become arbitrary file reads, permanent follow, or retry authority.

architecture_decisions.decision: Keep `.agents/skills/launchdeck-agent` canonical; route MCP-first only after capabilities/observation and allow CLI fallback only before mutation dispatch when transport/operation is unavailable.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Business refusal or uncertain work must never trigger another surface.

architecture_decisions.decision: Build one Node 20 ESM stdio runtime with esbuild and no external npm packages, then render separate policy-free Codex and Claude Plugin artifacts.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Hosts relocate artifacts and have different path/manifest rules while functional identity stays shared.

architecture_decisions.decision: Use one compatibility manifest with independent version axes, SemVer ranges, canonical component digests, and a non-self-referential SHA-256 build identity.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Package version or path alone cannot prove write compatibility.

architecture_decisions.decision: Keep Plugin and MCP lifetime independent from managed services and all shared state; update/disable/uninstall never stop tasks, migrate/delete state, or prune recovery evidence.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Compatible newer Plugins and CLI must take over older runs safely.

architecture_decisions.decision: Represent readiness as exact build/surface/operation/host/version/OS/scope/scenario evidence cells.
architecture_decisions.rationale: CLI or generator tests cannot prove MCP, host, update, uninstall, recovery, or platform readiness.

architecture_decisions.decision: Implement `adoption.inspect` as a bounded zero-write query and expose no `adoption.apply` transition.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Inspection must not write configuration, register a project, start a task, or authorize later apply.

implementation_sequence.purpose: Land catalog/result/compatibility schemas, canonical digest fixtures, Kernel interfaces, and contract validation before moving behavior.
Implement project/policy/compatibility pipeline, journal/recovery, observations, task/clean/adoption handlers, and converge lock ownership.
Route existing CLI through Kernel incrementally while keeping schemaVersion 1 compatibility and CLI-only routes.
Project the 18 tools over real stdio and prove schema/error/output/concurrency/recovery behavior.
Update canonical Skill routing and non-destructive current/legacy/Plugin digest diagnosis.
Generate isolated Codex/Claude artifacts, compatibility/build identity, integrity sidecars, and lifecycle-safe installers.
Run operation, CLI/MCP equivalence, fault, Skill trace, bundle, host/update/uninstall/takeover, and exact OS readiness gates.

interface_map.boundary: Declaration only; no state reads, effects, transport, or rendering.
Only query/execute/recover authority; adapters cannot mutate or decide safety.
Operation progress/effects evidence only; existing run/resource stores remain truth.
Argument mapping, schemaVersion 1 projection, rendering, exit codes; broader legacy routes remain CLI-only.
Protocol/schema adapter over exactly 18 public operations; no policy or Plugin-cwd targeting.
Intent/routing state machine; no raw controls, model approvals, policy, or retry invention.
Identity, ranges, digests, host paths; no duplicate lifecycle policy.
Exact cells only; no unsupported roll-up.

global_constraints: F:/github/launchdeck is the locked implementation target and current behavior authority.
The existing daemonless user-scoped Launchdeck control plane remains authoritative.
CLI, MCP, Skill, and Plugin runtimes share LAUNCHDECK_HOME, registry, run index, locks, logs, events, and operation journal.
Application Kernel policy is the only mutation authority; MCP annotations and host approvals are defense in depth only.
CLI schemaVersion 1 remains backward compatible while Agent protocol versions independently.
First-release MCP is local stdio, operates only on configured projects, and executes only operations whose current Kernel-evaluated risk is low.
Public Agent tools do not accept arbitrary command, env, cwd, force, risky cleanup, reset, or model-authored confirmation fields.
Node.js 20+ is a declared prerequisite; Plugins do not require a global CLI, npx, or install scripts.
Codex and Claude Plugins are separate generated artifacts from one compatibility manifest and build identity.
MCP-server and Plugin-host lifetime never owns managed-task lifetime.
Unknown or external process ownership is inspect-only.
No readiness claim may exceed its exact surface, host, operating-system, update, uninstall, and recovery evidence.
Pin MCP SDK v1.x, an SDK-compatible Zod, esbuild, and node-semver exactly in package-lock.json.
The bundled stdio artifact must contain no external npm-package import and must launch from a relocatable isolated directory with only Node 20+.
Operation Registry schemas and result/compatibility JSON Schemas use draft 2020-12.
The existing registry, run index, runtime state, ownership, events, logs, and process/port adapters remain resource truth.
acceptance_contracts: AC-001: For every shared operation contract case, CLI and MCP produce semantically equivalent operation, outcome, resource, effects, safety, error, next-action, and provenance data while preserving the existing CLI envelope.
AC-002: A Plugin-started service survives host and MCP exit, remains visible from standalone CLI, and can be safely stopped from that CLI when ownership and compatibility checks pass.
AC-003: Repeating start for the same configured project/task returns the existing run with reusedExistingRun=true and does not create a second process.
AC-004: A port or PID with unknown or external ownership can be inspected but every mutation is refused without Launchdeck ownership proof.
AC-005: Public MCP refuses medium, high, destructive, arbitrary-shell, external-process, and model-authored-confirmation execution even when a host annotation or approval suggests otherwise.

AC-006: Every mutation has an operation ID persisted before side effects; after a transport failure the caller can retrieve/reconcile by a known ID or use bounded operation.list correlation when the response carrying the ID was lost, and an indeterminate operation is never automatically retried or replayed through another surface.

AC-007: Reuse of an existing operation ID with the same input digest returns or reconciles the original result; a mismatched digest or expired/missing recovery record is refused without new execution.
AC-008: Safe clean executes only after the Kernel recomputes the target set and matches a current plan digest; preview drift is refused.
AC-009: Every MCP operation resolves project context explicitly and returns a missing, ambiguous, or scope-violation error rather than defaulting to a Plugin installation directory.

AC-010: Plugin update, disable, and uninstall leave managed services, registry, journal, logs, events, and shared configuration intact; a compatible newer Plugin and standalone CLI observe the same run ID, PID, port, and state home and can safely stop the older run without duplication.
AC-011: Capabilities reports actual runtime, protocol, state, schema, component digests, supported operations, risk limits, host, and project scope; component mismatch fails writes closed.

AC-012: The canonical Skill selects MCP first only for supported safe operations, uses CLI fallback only for unavailable or unsupported safe transport, and never falls back after a business refusal or uncertain mutation.

AC-013: Codex Skill installation checks both legacy ~/.codex/skills and current $HOME/.agents/skills locations, reports identical duplicates or divergence, and never overwrites or deletes divergent user content automatically.
AC-014: A real stdio protocol client verifies initialize, tools/list, tools/call, structured output, schema errors, stdout purity, stderr diagnostics, interruption, restart, and concurrency.

AC-015: Separate real Codex and Claude host smokes verify installation, routing, project scope, MCP launch, update, disable/uninstall, cross-version run identity, and shared-state behavior on every platform claimed ready.

AC-016: adoption.inspect returns a bounded proposed configuration plan without writing project or user configuration, registering a project, starting a task, or authorizing a later adoption.apply operation.

capability_operations.signal: Kernel operation registry and normalized result contract
capability_operations.operation: Execute declared Launchdeck operations through one Kernel-owned registry and normalized result contract.
Resolve projectRef, explicit projectRoot, host-provided project root, and registered user-scope projects with deterministic precedence and scope errors.
Evaluate current configured risk, ownership proof, project scope, and version compatibility before every mutation.
Journal each mutation by Kernel-issued operation ID and input digest, recover known work, and reconcile uncertain effects without blind replay.
Route current CLI commands through the Kernel while preserving schemaVersion 1 and independently exposing capabilities.
Expose typed Agent tools generated or validated from declared operations over stdout-pure local stdio MCP.
Expose capabilities, diagnosis, project/task inspection, status, and cursor/limit-bounded logs and events without arbitrary paths or permanent follow calls.
Start, stop, restart, and run only declared configured tasks whose current Kernel policy is low risk and whose ownership/scope checks pass.
Inspect/reconcile known operation IDs and execute safe clean only from a freshly revalidated Kernel plan digest.
Inspect candidate adoption configuration and return a plan without writing configuration, registering a project, authorizing apply, or starting a task.

capability_operations.signal: Canonical Skill routing and safe fallback
capability_operations.operation: Map lifecycle intent to declared operations, observe before mutation, refuse unsafe raw control, and apply the supported MCP-to-CLI fallback state machine.

capability_operations.signal: Compatibility manifest and dual host packaging
capability_operations.operation: Generate separately installable Codex and Claude artifacts with bundled runtime, canonical Skill, host metadata, and shared component digests.

capability_operations.signal: Protocol, fault, Skill-eval, host, update, uninstall, and platform verification
capability_operations.operation: Produce surface-specific automated and real-host evidence without unsupported readiness roll-up.
Future adoption.apply must consume a separately reviewed Kernel plan digest, revalidate it, and return before any task start.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: The user requires adoption writes in the first release.

capability_operations.operation: Future medium-risk execution requires declared client elicitation, human-response evidence, digest binding, and execution-time revalidation.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: A target host proves a reliable portable human elicitation contract and first-release scope changes.

capability_operations.signal: Remote MCP, OAuth, hooks, MCP UI, GUI/TUI, native packaging, and enterprise policy
capability_operations.operation: No first-release implementation or readiness claim; each surface requires a later specification and evidence boundary.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: Any listed surface becomes a required delivery outcome.

capability_operations.signal: High/destructive public MCP, force-stop, project removal, risky clean, reset, arbitrary shell/env/cwd/force, and permanent follow
capability_operations.operation: The public Agent surface must not expose these operations or inputs.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: A new reviewed safety contract explicitly adds one of these capabilities.

capability_operations.operation: Unknown or external-owned processes remain inspect-only.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: Ownership semantics are explicitly redefined in a new discussion.

capability_operations.operation: Use resource-aware idempotency, journal recovery, and explicit indeterminate outcomes instead.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: A restricted operation class can prove stronger semantics without changing the general promise.

capability_operations.operation: The first-release local control plane must not target production or remote managed services.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: The user requires production or remote service management under a new trust, authentication, policy, and evidence contract.

capability_preservation.surface_minimization_policy: Remap preserved operations to an executable entry point or reference a user-confirmed deferral; do not delete capability.

entries.required_implementation: Execute declared Launchdeck operations through one Kernel-owned registry and normalized result contract.
Resolve projectRef, explicit projectRoot, host-provided project root, and registered user-scope projects with deterministic precedence and scope errors.
Evaluate current configured risk, ownership proof, project scope, and version compatibility before every mutation.
Journal each mutation by Kernel-issued operation ID and input digest, recover known work, and reconcile uncertain effects without blind replay.
Route current CLI commands through the Kernel while preserving schemaVersion 1 and independently exposing capabilities.

entries.selected_entry_point: public MCP operation projection and shared Kernel
entries.required_implementation: Expose typed Agent tools generated or validated from declared operations over stdout-pure local stdio MCP.

entries.selected_entry_point: public MCP operation projection and shared Kernel
entries.required_implementation: Expose capabilities, diagnosis, project/task inspection, status, and cursor/limit-bounded logs and events without arbitrary paths or permanent follow calls.

entries.selected_entry_point: public MCP operation projection and shared Kernel
entries.required_implementation: Start, stop, restart, and run only declared configured tasks whose current Kernel policy is low risk and whose ownership/scope checks pass.

entries.selected_entry_point: public MCP operation projection and shared Kernel
entries.required_implementation: Inspect/reconcile known operation IDs and execute safe clean only from a freshly revalidated Kernel plan digest.

entries.selected_entry_point: public MCP operation projection and shared Kernel
entries.required_implementation: Inspect candidate adoption configuration and return a plan without writing configuration, registering a project, authorizing apply, or starting a task.
Map lifecycle intent to declared operations, observe before mutation, refuse unsafe raw control, and apply the supported MCP-to-CLI fallback state machine.
Generate separately installable Codex and Claude artifacts with bundled runtime, canonical Skill, host metadata, and shared component digests.
Produce surface-specific automated and real-host evidence without unsupported readiness roll-up.
Future adoption.apply must consume a separately reviewed Kernel plan digest, revalidate it, and return before any task start.
entries.reopen_or_conflict_condition: The user requires adoption writes in the first release.

entries.required_implementation: Future medium-risk execution requires declared client elicitation, human-response evidence, digest binding, and execution-time revalidation.
entries.reopen_or_conflict_condition: A target host proves a reliable portable human elicitation contract and first-release scope changes.

entries.required_implementation: No first-release implementation or readiness claim; each surface requires a later specification and evidence boundary.
entries.reopen_or_conflict_condition: Any listed surface becomes a required delivery outcome.

entries.required_implementation: The public Agent surface must not expose these operations or inputs.
entries.reopen_or_conflict_condition: A new reviewed safety contract explicitly adds one of these capabilities.

entries.required_implementation: Unknown or external-owned processes remain inspect-only.
entries.reopen_or_conflict_condition: Ownership semantics are explicitly redefined in a new discussion.

entries.required_implementation: Use resource-aware idempotency, journal recovery, and explicit indeterminate outcomes instead.
entries.reopen_or_conflict_condition: A restricted operation class can prove stronger semantics without changing the general promise.

entries.required_implementation: The first-release local control plane must not target production or remote managed services.
entries.reopen_or_conflict_condition: The user requires production or remote service management under a new trust, authentication, policy, and evidence contract.

must_preserve.claim: Launchdeck remains one authoritative local lifecycle control plane across CLI, Skill, MCP, and Plugins.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: The specification must not create a second backend, state namespace, or safety authority.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any surface becomes an independent lifecycle authority.

must_preserve.claim: F:/github/launchdeck is the implementation target and current behavior authority.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: All implementation and verification paths remain bound to the current repository.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Implementation moves to another repository or package boundary.

must_preserve.claim: First release is configured-project, low-risk, local stdio MCP with one canonical Skill and dual generated host Plugins.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Scope and acceptance criteria preserve this release boundary.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A deferred risk, adoption, remote, UI, native, or enterprise surface becomes mandatory.

must_preserve.claim: CLI compatibility and Agent protocol are separate versioned contracts.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Specification preserves CLI schemaVersion 1 while defining a normalized Agent Operation Result.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Agent semantics require breaking existing CLI consumers.

must_preserve.claim: One canonical launchdeck-agent Skill remains intent-driven, reference-based, MCP-first, and safety-preserving.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Do not split first release into competing public lifecycle Skills or allow blind fallback.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Users must select low-level tools or duplicate Skill copies drift.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin installation, update, disable, and uninstall are independent from managed-service and shared-state lifetime.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Acceptance criteria prove services and state survive host and Plugin lifecycle changes.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin lifecycle can implicitly stop services or remove state.

must_preserve.claim: Mutation recovery is journaled and evidence-based; indeterminate work is not automatically retried.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Specification includes operation IDs, journal state, retry semantics, and fault-injection acceptance.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A transport failure can replay mutation through MCP or CLI.

must_preserve.claim: Release readiness is scoped by surface, host, OS, update, uninstall, and recovery evidence.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: The spec defines separate readiness claims and prevents CLI evidence from proving Plugin readiness.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A broad ready claim lacks exact supporting evidence.

ui_design_contract.surface_type: CLI, Skill, MCP protocol, and Plugin packaging behavior
ui_design_contract.subject: Local project lifecycle control
ui_design_contract.single_job: Express a lifecycle intent and receive a safe, observable, recoverable Launchdeck result without choosing unsafe low-level process controls.
ui_design_contract.content_thesis: Reports name the project, task, executing surface/runtime, outcome, resource status, effects certainty, evidence, refusal reason, and safe next action.
ui_design_contract.interaction_thesis: Observe before mutate; reuse matching runs; stop on refusal or uncertainty; reconcile before any retry.
experience_requirements: Users express lifecycle intent without naming low-level Launchdeck tools.
Agents observe before mutation and report an existing run instead of duplicating it.
Failures identify project, task, runtime/surface, evidence, refusal reason, and a safe next action.
Plugin and host lifecycle never unexpectedly stops a managed local service or deletes recovery evidence.
must_preserve: Structured error and safe next-action semantics
may_adapt: MCP host-specific tool presentation and manifest metadata
must_not: Imply deferred UI/TUI or remote control surfaces

minimal_live_reads: package.json and package-lock.json for Node/ESM/dependency/package boundaries
src/cli.js and src/output.js for current orchestration and schemaVersion 1
src/config.js and schema/launchdeck.schema.json for task risk/config schema
src/global-runtime.js, src/runtime.js, and src/control-plane/ for registry/run/locks/ownership/events/resource truth
src/agent-installer.js and .agents/skills/launchdeck-agent/ for canonical Skill/install behavior
validation_routes: CLI schemaVersion 1 fixtures and CLI/MCP semantic-equivalence suites
journal crash-point, retention, recovery, correlation, and no-replay suites
separate real Codex and Claude install/update/disable/uninstall/cross-version takeover smokes
stale_if: The source discussion review digest changes
Any retained live evidence path changes before planning
The implementation target moves outside F:/github/launchdeck
A target host cannot launch the bundled runtime under Node.js 20+ and the generated manifest model
A deferred surface becomes first-release scope
Official MCP SDK production recommendation or host Plugin path/manifest contract changes.
Any retained live evidence path changes before task compilation.
A new external npm/runtime asset remains in the isolated bundle.
Project cognition is rebuilt and contradicts a live-evidence planning claim.

cognition_status.reason: project-cognition.db is missing; live repository evidence was used as authority.
cognition_status.planning_effect: non-blocking advisory; no graph-only implementation claim is accepted.

reasons: Process ownership and destructive clean boundaries
Readiness claims across hosts and operating systems

state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Return the existing run with reusedExistingRun=true; do not spawn.
Permit inspection only and refuse mutation.
Return possible or unknown effects certainty and require operation.get or operation.reconcile; never retry automatically.

state_behavior_matrix.state: response lost before the caller observes the Kernel-issued ID
state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Use bounded operation.list correlation by project/task/name/time/state; reconcile only one unique candidate and stop on zero or ambiguous matches.
Return or recover the original operation without re-execution.

state_behavior_matrix.state: same ID, different digest or missing retained record

state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Refuse execution and return a new plan requirement.
Managed services and shared state remain; compatible standalone CLI can inspect and control them.
Preserve all content, report both digests, and require explicit user-selected migration.
Allow safe diagnosis/inspection where possible and fail writes closed.

dependency_impact.dependency: Registry, run index, locks, ownership, events, logs, runtime state, process and port adapters
dependency_impact.impact: Kernel extraction must reuse their truth and preserve daemonless lifecycle semantics.
dependency_impact.validation: Existing and new control-plane contract tests plus adapter-equivalence cases.

dependency_impact.impact: Kernel and normalized Agent results cannot break existing JSON automation.
dependency_impact.validation: Backward-compatibility fixtures and semantic mapping tests.

dependency_impact.dependency: MCP stdio framing and structured results
dependency_impact.impact: Non-protocol stdout, unbounded calls, or schema drift breaks every host.

dependency_impact.dependency: Codex and Claude host path resolution
dependency_impact.impact: One host manifest or path assumption cannot prove the other host works.
dependency_impact.validation: Separate real-host install, routing, update, disable/uninstall, and shared-state smokes.

dependency_impact.impact: Skill/runtime/schema drift can issue unsupported or unsafe operations.

recovery_and_validation: Locks and operation IDs make safe retries resource-aware and idempotent; they do not imply universal exactly-once execution.
Transport failure returns a recoverable known result or an explicit indeterminate result backed by journal and live evidence.
Rollback never terminates unknown/external processes or deletes shared state as a side effect of Plugin lifecycle.
Journal, compatibility, and manifest migrations must be versioned, fail writes closed on incompatibility, and retain diagnosis paths.
Acceptance evidence must include positive behavior, refusal behavior, interruption/restart, concurrency, update/uninstall, and takeover/recovery.
coverage_gaps: Project cognition graph store is missing; architecture ownership claims must be rechecked against live files during task compilation and implementation.
No journal, canonical input digest, stable observation cursor, compatibility/readiness evidence store, or component manifest implementation exists yet.
Current synchronous and asynchronous lifecycle lock paths must converge before Kernel mutation cutover.
Current installer force behavior does not satisfy automatic non-destructive divergent-copy handling.
Codex relative path behavior, Claude root substitution, isolated bundling, Node resolution, lifecycle independence, and cross-version coexistence remain claim-gated by real evidence.
The safe-clean drift outcome code is consolidated as plan_digest_mismatch before fixture freeze.

consequence_obligations.claim: CLI and MCP share the same safety Kernel; MCP cannot bypass ownership proof.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any MCP handler mutates state outside Kernel.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Map every mutation adapter to the same Kernel policy and ownership checks.

consequence_obligations.claim: MCP annotations are hints; deterministic Kernel controls provide real authorization.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Tool annotations authorize mutation.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Annotations never replace policy, ownership, risk, or confirmation checks.

consequence_obligations.claim: Starting an already matching task returns its existing run instead of duplicating it.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Repeated start can spawn a second service.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Specify duplicate detection and reusedExistingRun semantics.

consequence_obligations.claim: External and unknown process owners remain inspect-only.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Port or PID alone authorizes mutation.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: All mutation tools refuse without Launchdeck ownership proof.

consequence_obligations.claim: Risky cleanup requires a plan/digest/confirmation boundary and is not exposed as a model-authored yes flag.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: MCP can trigger risky deletion with a boolean input.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: First release exposes only digest-bound safe clean and defers risky clean.

consequence_obligations.claim: MCP logs/events are bounded reads or polling, never permanent follow calls.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A tool call can remain open indefinitely.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Schemas include bounds/cursors and exclude arbitrary paths or unbounded streams.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugin runtime and standalone CLI verify state/schema compatibility and fail closed for unsafe writes.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: An older runtime writes newer shared state.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Capabilities report actual runtime and compatibility before mutation.

consequence_obligations.claim: Task risk is dynamically enforced by Kernel, not inferred only from static MCP annotation.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Configured risk remains display-only.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Every operation resolves current configured risk before execution.

consequence_obligations.claim: Every MCP operation explicitly resolves project context and never defaults to Plugin installation directory.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: An operation can target the wrong directory implicitly.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Define projectRef/projectRoot/host-root precedence and missing/ambiguous errors.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugin runtime, standalone CLI, registry, and run state use explicit compatibility checks and incompatible writes fail closed.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Package version alone decides write safety.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Separate package, Agent protocol, output, config, registry, and journal version axes.

consequence_obligations.claim: An uncertain MCP mutation result cannot automatically fall back to CLI retry.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Transport failure can replay mutation.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Fallback state machine inspects/reconciles uncertain work before any new operation.

consequence_obligations.claim: All operation results carry execution provenance.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Users cannot identify which runtime executed an action.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Results identify surface, host, runtime kind/version/path, state home, and protocol version as applicable.

consequence_obligations.claim: Safe clean is bound to a revalidated plan digest.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Changed targets can be deleted under an old preview.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Kernel recomputes the plan and rejects preview/execute drift.

consequence_obligations.claim: Agent task tools never accept arbitrary command, cwd, env, or force parameters.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: MCP becomes a general shell wrapper.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Tool schemas select only declared projects/tasks and bounded options.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugin update and uninstall never migrate or delete shared control-plane state implicitly.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin lifecycle mutates shared state.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: State changes require explicit Launchdeck lifecycle/state operations.

consequence_obligations.claim: Task risk becomes a Kernel execution gate shared by CLI, MCP, and Plugins.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any surface bypasses task risk policy.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Current display-only risk gap is closed at the application boundary.

consequence_obligations.claim: Host approval and MCP annotations do not replace Kernel risk decisions.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Host configuration elevates Kernel permissions.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Policy remains deterministic even when hosts auto-approve tools.

consequence_obligations.claim: Medium risk requires declared client elicitation and an accepted response; first release defers this capability.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Medium-risk execution becomes first-release scope.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Public MCP v1 rejects medium risk while preserving a future capability-negotiated contract.

consequence_obligations.claim: Future confirmation binds to an operation digest and execution revalidates target, lock, port, and ownership.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A confirmed operation can execute changed inputs or state.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Preserve prepare/confirm/revalidate semantics even though medium risk is deferred.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugin installation scope and executable project scope are distinct; project Plugins cannot cross project roots.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Project Plugin can target unrelated projects.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Define project-scope violations and registered-project rules for user scope.

consequence_obligations.claim: MCP server lifetime does not control managed-task lifetime.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Closing the host implicitly stops a managed service.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Host/MCP exit leaves Launchdeck-owned services running and observable.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugin disable, update, or uninstall preserves shared control-plane state.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin removal destroys recovery evidence.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Host lifecycle tests verify registry, journal, logs, events, config, and services remain.

consequence_obligations.claim: Standalone and Plugin Skills detect duplicate installation and content drift.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Two Skills issue conflicting routing or safety guidance.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Installer doctor compares canonical content digests without overwriting divergent user files.

consequence_obligations.claim: Normalized results separate operation outcome from resource lifecycle status.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: One status field ambiguously means protocol and lifecycle state.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Define unambiguous outcome.kind and resource.status fields.

consequence_obligations.claim: CLI envelope and Agent protocol are independently versioned.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A protocol change silently breaks CLI automation.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Compatibility checks do not conflate package, CLI, Agent, config, registry, or journal versions.

consequence_obligations.claim: A plan digest is integrity evidence, not a user-confirmation proof.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Possessing a digest authorizes mutation.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Specification separates plan validation from human interaction evidence.

consequence_obligations.claim: MCP inputs cannot contain model-forgeable confirmation fields.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: The model can self-assert human approval.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Exclude confirmed/yes/approval token fields from public tool schemas.

consequence_obligations.claim: Every mutation is operation-journaled for retry and recovery safety.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A Server restart loses mutation certainty.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Define journal states, operation IDs, input digest, result references, and retention.

consequence_obligations.claim: Indeterminate foreground task execution is never automatically rerun.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A possibly side-effecting task runs twice.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: task.run recovery distinguishes unknown completion from safe retry.

consequence_obligations.claim: Future adoption writes can only apply a Kernel-generated and revalidated adoption plan.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Automatic adoption writes enter first-release scope.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: First release keeps adoption inspect-only and preserves safe deferred semantics.

consequence_obligations.claim: Adoption and task start are distinct mutations and cannot be implicitly chained.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Configuration acceptance automatically launches a task.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Read-only discovery never starts a server; future apply returns before start.

consequence_obligations.claim: npm runtime and host Plugins are different artifacts generated from one compatibility manifest.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Artifact metadata or behavior drifts between builds.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Define one build identity and artifact-specific packaging contracts.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugins carry a prebuilt MCP runtime and do not depend on global CLI, npx, or install-time scripts.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin installation requires arbitrary code execution or PATH resolution.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Plugin startup command points to bundled runtime with bundled dependencies.

consequence_obligations.claim: First-release installability explicitly requires Node.js 20+.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Product claims zero-runtime-dependency installability without native binaries.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Plugin prerequisites and diagnostics report Node compatibility clearly.

consequence_obligations.claim: Skill, runtime, schemas, compatibility manifest, and Plugin manifest share one build identity and digests.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: New instructions drive an old runtime or schema.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Capabilities fail writes on component digest mismatch.

consequence_obligations.claim: MCP stdio stdout contains only valid protocol messages.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any non-protocol text contaminates stdout framing.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: All banners, diagnostics, and task output go to stderr or bounded state/log channels.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugin update, disable, and uninstall do not affect managed services or shared state.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Host package lifecycle changes product runtime state.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Update/uninstall acceptance tests prove lifetime separation.

consequence_obligations.claim: Host manifests do not duplicate risk, safety, state, or operation semantics.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A host manifest becomes a conflicting policy source.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Manifests contain identity, component paths, metadata, prerequisites, and presentation only.

consequence_obligations.claim: CLI cross-platform smoke does not prove MCP or Plugin cross-platform compatibility.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A Plugin claim relies only on CLI smoke.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Define separate surface/host/platform evidence matrices.

consequence_obligations.claim: Codex and Claude Plugins are independently verified before cross-host readiness.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: One host's smoke is generalized to another.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Each host has installation, routing, MCP, scope, update, and uninstall evidence.

consequence_obligations.claim: Adapter equivalence compares normalized semantics rather than raw host formatting.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Brittle snapshots replace semantic contract proof.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Shared contract cases compare operation, outcome, resource, effects, safety, errors, next actions, and provenance.

consequence_obligations.claim: Launchdeck does not claim universal exactly-once mutation semantics.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Specification promises impossible exactly-once behavior.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Product language distinguishes recoverable idempotency from indeterminate side effects.

consequence_obligations.claim: Retrying the same operation ID returns or recovers the original operation without re-execution.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Same-ID retry can repeat side effects.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Journal state and recovery logic are keyed by operation ID and input digest.

consequence_obligations.claim: Indeterminate mutation cannot be automatically retried or replayed across surfaces.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Uncertain work is silently attempted again.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Skill, CLI, and MCP all stop and inspect/reconcile when certainty is unavailable.

consequence_obligations.claim: Skill evaluation verifies actual tool-call sequences and forbidden calls, not only final prose.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A plausible answer hides unsafe calls.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Eval cases assert MCP-first selection, safe fallback, refusal handling, and no raw process control.

consequence_obligations.claim: MCP release verification uses real stdio framing.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Only direct handler tests support an MCP-ready claim.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Automated protocol clients cover initialize, tools/list, tools/call, schema, output, stderr/stdout, interruption, restart, and concurrency.

consequence_obligations.claim: A new Plugin version observes and safely manages services started by an older compatible version.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Update duplicates or loses an existing run.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Update acceptance binds run ID, PID, port, state home, and compatible stop behavior across versions.

consequence_obligations.claim: Plugin uninstall preserves services and shared state and standalone CLI can take over.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Uninstall strands or deletes managed state.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Uninstall acceptance covers service liveness, state/log/event presence, CLI inspect/log/stop, and reinstall recovery.

consequence_obligations.claim: Codex and Claude each require real host acceptance rather than manifest-only mocks.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A host-ready claim lacks a real installation/use trace.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Host-specific smoke is a named release gate.

consequence_obligations.claim: Release claims bind to exact surface, host, OS, and verification evidence.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Marketing or docs claim broader readiness than verified.
consequence_obligations.downstream_requirement: Define layered readiness labels and prohibit unsupported roll-up claims.

operational_consequence_decisions.affected_boundary: Kernel, registry, CLI/MCP adapters, normalized result, compatibility
operational_consequence_decisions.state_machine_ordering: Resolve the declared operation and trusted context, validate compatibility/scope, journal mutations, acquire locks, revalidate policy, execute once, normalize one result, then adapt presentation.
operational_consequence_decisions.concurrency_idempotency: Adapters cannot own mutation paths; Kernel locks and the operation ID/digest contract serialize shared effects.
operational_consequence_decisions.recovery_path: Fail writes closed on incompatible or uncertain state; return bounded diagnosis or journal reconciliation without replay.

operational_consequence_decisions.affected_boundary: Configured project, task, run, PID, port, ownership and host/project scope
operational_consequence_decisions.state_machine_ordering: Resolve exactly one configured project, load current task/run evidence, acquire project/task locks, re-read ownership and ports, then reuse or mutate.
operational_consequence_decisions.concurrency_idempotency: Matching starts reuse one verified run; PID/port alone never authorizes mutation; host/Plugin lifetime never owns task lifetime.
operational_consequence_decisions.recovery_path: Unknown/external/conflicting targets remain inspect-only; stale or partial states go to explicit reconcile.

operational_consequence_decisions.affected_boundary: Operation journal, operation/input IDs, effects certainty, safe-clean plan
operational_consequence_decisions.state_machine_ordering: Persist prepared before effects, enter running under locks after revalidation, then terminate or become indeterminate; reconcile never invokes the original handler.
operational_consequence_decisions.concurrency_idempotency: Same ID/same digest recovers original; digest mismatch or missing record refuses; clean apply recomputes under lock and returns plan_digest_mismatch for drift.
operational_consequence_decisions.recovery_path: Known ID uses get/reconcile; a lost ID uses one bounded correlation query; zero/multiple candidates stop and no surface retries uncertain work.

operational_consequence_decisions.affected_boundary: Canonical Skill, compatibility/build identity, Codex/Claude artifacts, lifecycle separation
operational_consequence_decisions.state_machine_ordering: Build one runtime and Skill identity, render two policy-free host packages, install non-destructively, and diagnose every observed component before mutation.
operational_consequence_decisions.concurrency_idempotency: Compatible old/new runtimes share LAUNCHDECK_HOME; Plugin lifecycle changes component presence only and never mutates managed tasks/state.
operational_consequence_decisions.recovery_path: Digest/version mismatch is diagnosis-only; divergent Skill copies are preserved until explicit user migration; compatible CLI takes over after uninstall.

operational_consequence_decisions.affected_boundary: Bounded observations, stdio protocol, Skill traces, host/OS readiness evidence
operational_consequence_decisions.state_machine_ordering: Initialize MCP, list projected tools, execute bounded calls, record exact build/host/OS evidence, and publish only passing cells.
operational_consequence_decisions.concurrency_idempotency: A single stdout writer preserves framing; cursors bind high-water snapshots; host cells never roll up across untested dimensions.
operational_consequence_decisions.recovery_path: Interruption/restart is tested over the real wire; failures withhold only the exact claim and expose evidence.

operational_consequence_decisions.state_machine_ordering: No first-release execute transition exists; adoption.inspect ends after a bounded read-only plan.
operational_consequence_decisions.concurrency_idempotency: Future confirmation must bind a Kernel plan/operation digest and revalidate target, lock, port, ownership, and current policy.
operational_consequence_decisions.recovery_path: Any demand to activate these transitions stops and reopens specification before implementation.

review_risk_notes.containment: Cut over by operation with schemaVersion 1 fixtures and normalized adapter-equivalence tests; do not delete CLI-only compatibility routes.

review_risk_notes.risk: Journal becomes a second run truth
review_risk_notes.containment: Persist references and effects evidence only; resource lifecycle remains in existing run/ownership/event/log authorities.
Prepared-before-effects, ID/digest recovery, explicit indeterminate outcomes, bounded correlation, and no cross-surface retry.

review_risk_notes.risk: Host packaging duplicates policy or state
review_risk_notes.containment: Policy-free templates, one build identity, isolated archive inspection, and real-host lifecycle tests.
Exact evidence cells bound to build/host/version/OS/scope/scenario; missing cells withhold only that claim.

stop_and_reopen_conditions: Any adapter or host package requires an independent lifecycle backend, state namespace, policy authority, or Plugin-owned task lifetime.
CLI schemaVersion 1 cannot preserve existing public field/status/compact/exit-code semantics while mapping Kernel results.
Any first-release public Agent tool requires medium/high/destructive execution, arbitrary shell/env/cwd/force, risky clean, external-process mutation, permanent follow, or remote/production control.
Adoption configuration writes or automatic task start become required.
A host cannot launch the isolated prebuilt runtime under the Node 20+ prerequisite without a global CLI, npx, install script, or Plugin-local node_modules.
Journal/lock design cannot persist prepared before effects or avoid automatic replay of indeterminate work.
Live repository evidence contradicts the retained ownership/state authority map.

validation.claim: Same Kernel semantics with presentation-specific envelopes.
MCP wire behavior for the tested build/OS only.
MCP-first safe routing and non-destructive migration.
Independent service/state lifetime and compatible takeover.

notes: Spec-first: planning consumes a planning-ready spec package with empty semantic_delta.
Reuse/simplicity: one Kernel extends existing resource/state owners; no second backend.
Test-backed: contract, fault, adapter, real stdio, Skill, bundle, host, and readiness evidence are mandatory.
Security: strict tool inputs, configured low-risk-only mutation, ownership proof, digest-bound clean, stdout purity, and fail-closed compatibility.
Reviewability/reversibility: incremental CLI cutover, versioned journal, deterministic artifacts, non-destructive Skill handling, and scoped readiness.

required_refs.purpose: Official host/runtime/dependency decisions and claim gates
State, journal, concurrency, retention, and recovery design
Agent result, operation, MCP, compatibility, and Plugin generation boundaries
Representative end-to-end validation and readiness matrix
